sections.heading: RT
sections.content: {"role": "Used for adjuvant treatment to improve locoregional control, definitive treatment for medically inoperable patients, and palliation for recurrent/metastatic disease.", "principles": ["RT is directed at sites of known or suspected tumor involvement and may include EBRT and/or brachytherapy.", "Imaging required to assess locoregional extent and rule out distant metastases before RT.", "EBRT typically targets pelvis with or without para-aortic region. Brachytherapy delivered to intact uterus preoperatively/definitively or to vagina post-hysterectomy.", "Whole abdominal radiotherapy is not considered tumor-directed RT and is no longer recommended due to toxicity.", "Chemoradiation can be given concurrently or sequentially.", "For uterine sarcomas, adjuvant RT reduces local recurrence but no consistent OS benefit; role controversial."], "dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Adjuvant Pelvic EBRT", "total_dose": "45–50 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8–2.0 Gy", "fractions": "25–28", "schedule": "Daily, 5 days per week.", "indication": "Microscopic disease in pelvis and para-aortic nodes for high-risk endometrial carcinoma or uterine sarcomas."}, {"name": "Boost for Gross Residual Disease", "total_dose": "60–70 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8–2.0 Gy", "fractions": "Additional 5–10", "schedule": "Sequential after pelvic EBRT.", "indication": "Postoperative gross residual disease or gross nodal disease (boost to 60–65 Gy)."}, {"name": "Neoadjuvant Radiation", "total_dose": "45–50 Gy ± HDR brachytherapy boost to total EQD2 75–80 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "EBRT: 1.8–2.0 Gy; HDR: 5–7 Gy per fraction", "fractions": "EBRT: 25–28; HDR: 1–2 insertions", "schedule": "Sequential.", "indication": "To minimize positive margins at hysterectomy for cervical involvement or locally advanced disease."}, {"name": "Postoperative Vaginal Brachytherapy Alone", "total_dose": "30–35 Gy EQD2", "dose_per_fraction": "5.5–7 Gy", "fractions": "3–5", "schedule": "Weekly or twice weekly.", "indication": "Adjuvant for low-intermediate risk endometrial carcinoma (e.g., Stage IA G1-2, Stage IB G1)."}, {"name": "Brachytherapy Boost to EBRT", "total_dose": "10–18 Gy EQD2", "dose_per_fraction": "4–6 Gy", "fractions": "2–3", "schedule": "Concurrent or sequential with EBRT.", "indication": "Boost to vaginal cuff after hysterectomy for higher risk features."}, {"name": "Definitive Brachytherapy for Medically Inoperable Uterine Cancer", "total_dose": "EQD2 D90 ≥48 Gy (brachytherapy alone) or ≥65 Gy (combined EBRT + brachytherapy); GTV target ≥80 Gy with MRI guidance.", "dose_per_fraction": "Individualized based on fractionation.", "fractions": "Multiple", "schedule": "Individualized.", "indication": "Medically inoperable uterine-confined carcinoma."}], "approaches": [{"name": "Intensity-Modulated Radiation Therapy (IMRT)", "dose_fractionation": "As per dose frameworks above.", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Cisplatin-based concurrent chemoradiation for high-risk disease (category 1 for some histologies).", "indication": "Pelvic and para-aortic RT for endometrial carcinoma and uterine sarcomas; preferred to minimize toxicity.", "key_trial": "PORTEC-3, GOG 258, RTOG 9708.", "toxicities": "Diarrhea, bowel symptoms, fatigue, secondary malignancies (long-term)."}, {"name": "High-Dose-Rate (HDR) Brachytherapy", "dose_fractionation": "As per dose frameworks above.", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Can be interdigitated with chemotherapy for carcinosarcoma.", "indication": "Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy, definitive treatment for medically inoperable disease.", "key_trial": "PORTEC-2, Aalders trial.", "toxicities": "Vaginal stenosis, bleeding, urinary symptoms."}, {"name": "Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy (SBRT)", "dose_fractionation": "1–5 fractions of ablative doses (e.g., 30–50 Gy in 1–5 fractions).", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Not typically concurrent.", "indication": "Oligometastatic disease (1–5 metastatic lesions) if primary cancer controlled.", "key_trial": "SABR-COMET.", "toxicities": "Site-specific (e.g., pneumonitis for lung, hepatotoxicity for liver)."}]}

gauges.label: Definitive Brachytherapy for Medically Inoperable Uterine Cancer

timeline.title: Adjuvant Pelvic EBRT
timeline.body: Total Dose: 45–50 Gy, Dose per Fraction: 1.8–2.0 Gy, Fractions: 25–28, Schedule: Daily, 5 days per week., Indication: Microscopic disease in pelvis and para-aortic nodes for high-risk endometrial carcinoma or uterine sarcomas.

timeline.title: Boost for Gross Residual Disease
timeline.body: Total Dose: 60–70 Gy, Dose per Fraction: 1.8–2.0 Gy, Fractions: Additional 5–10, Schedule: Sequential after pelvic EBRT., Indication: Postoperative gross residual disease or gross nodal disease (boost to 60–65 Gy).

timeline.title: Neoadjuvant Radiation
timeline.body: Total Dose: 45–50 Gy ± HDR brachytherapy boost to total EQD2 75–80 Gy, Dose per Fraction: EBRT: 1.8–2.0 Gy; HDR: 5–7 Gy per fraction, Fractions: EBRT: 25–28; HDR: 1–2 insertions, Schedule: Sequential., Indication: To minimize positive margins at hysterectomy for cervical involvement or locally advanced disease.

timeline.title: Postoperative Vaginal Brachytherapy Alone
timeline.body: Total Dose: 30–35 Gy EQD2, Dose per Fraction: 5.5–7 Gy, Fractions: 3–5, Schedule: Weekly or twice weekly., Indication: Adjuvant for low-intermediate risk endometrial carcinoma (e.g., Stage IA G1-2, Stage IB G1).

timeline.title: Brachytherapy Boost to EBRT
timeline.body: Total Dose: 10–18 Gy EQD2, Dose per Fraction: 4–6 Gy, Fractions: 2–3, Schedule: Concurrent or sequential with EBRT., Indication: Boost to vaginal cuff after hysterectomy for higher risk features.

timeline.title: Definitive Brachytherapy for Medically Inoperable Uterine Cancer
timeline.body: Total Dose: EQD2 D90 ≥48 Gy (brachytherapy alone) or ≥65 Gy (combined EBRT + brachytherapy); GTV target ≥80 Gy with MRI guidance., Dose per Fraction: Individualized based on fractionation., Fractions: Multiple, Schedule: Individualized., Indication: Medically inoperable uterine-confined carcinoma.
